preamble: The shift from the voiced dental fricative [ð] to the voiced labiodental fricative [v] is a specific form of 'th-fronting'. In this process, the point of articulation moves forward from the teeth to the lips while maintaining the fricative manner.

examples.note: Th-fronting occurs frequently in word-medial position, merging the dental fricative with the labiodental fricative.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Wells, J. C. (1982). Accents of English.
